mainEntity.name: What if I can never go back to driving a forklift or working in a warehouse because of my injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: You may be eligible for vocational rehabilitation and permanent disability benefits. If your injury prevents you from returning to your old job, the workers\' comp system should provide support for retraining or compensate you for your lost earning potential.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If your forklift injury was severe, you may reach "Maximum Medical Improvement" (MMI) but still have permanent limitations, such as an inability to lift more than 20 pounds or sit for long periods. If your old warehouse job requires heavy lifting, you may be eligible for vocational rehabilitation or "permanent disability" payments to compensate for your lost earning capacity.

In Utah and Idaho, the system is designed to provide you with a percentage of your wages while you retrain for a new career that fits your physical abilities.

Long-term possibilities:
- Permanent Partial Disability (PPD) payments based on impairment.
- Vocational retraining for a non-physical desk job.
- Lump-sum settlements to cover future lost wages.

Do not let your employer pressure you into "pushing through" the pain. If you can\'t safely do the job, your doctor needs to put that in writing.

A lawyer is essential for negotiating a settlement that accounts for the fact that you can no longer work in your chosen field for the next 20 years.
